name: Rosa Ponselle
abstract: Rosa Ponzillo, known as Rosa Ponselle (January 22, 1897 – May 25, 1981) was an American operatic dramatic soprano. She sang mainly at the New York Metropolitan Opera and is generally considered to have been one of the greatest sopranos of the 20th century.

works.t: Oberon: Act II. Ozean, du Ungeheuer
Mefistofele: Act III. L'altra notte in fondo al mare

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Williams
about_quotes.evidence: And Rosa Ponselle, who used to do “Carmen” I remember. And of course on the radio we had the Metropolitan every Saturday.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Williams interviewed by Leslie Gourse, New York City, New York, March 9, 1996
